# Smithy Eval Fixture

This is a minimal Express TypeScript API project used as the **reference fixture** for Smithy eval scenarios. It is **not a real application** — it exists solely to give eval agents a concrete, stable codebase with specific file paths, route patterns, and data types that plans can reference.

## Project Structure

- `src/index.ts` — Express app entry point. Mounts the users router at `/api/users` and starts the server on a configurable `PORT`.
- `src/types.ts` — TypeScript interfaces (`User`, `CreateUserRequest`) used by route handlers.
- `src/routes/users.ts` — Express Router with three user CRUD handlers: list, get by id, and create.
- `package.json` — Node project manifest with Express as a runtime dependency, and TypeScript, `@types/express`, and `@types/node` as dev dependencies.
- `tsconfig.json` — TypeScript config targeting ES2022 with CommonJS module resolution.

## Intentional Gap

The app intentionally has **no health check endpoint**. This gap is the primary eval prompt target — eval scenarios ask agents to "add a health check endpoint" and validate that the resulting plan references the specific files and structures in this fixture.

## Planted Inconsistencies

In addition to the intentional gap above, the fixture contains **deliberate, scout-detectable inconsistencies** that exist solely to exercise smithy-scout coverage for **User Story 8 (US8)** of the evals framework spec (`specs/2026-04-06-003-smithy-evals-framework/08-fixture-deliberate-inconsistencies-for-scout.tasks.md`). **Do not "clean up" these plants.** Future maintainers, `smithy-fix`, and `smithy-scout` runs at deeper depths must leave them in place — removing them silently breaks the US8 scout scenario, which asserts that at least one Warning or Conflict finding is surfaced against this fixture.

Each plant maps to a row in smithy-scout's Severity Guidelines table (see `src/templates/agent-skills/agents/smithy.scout.prompt`).

| File | Location | Plant | Expected scout category |
|------|----------|-------|-------------------------|
| `evals/fixture/src/routes/users.ts` | Line 14 — the `// GET /:id — get user by email address` comment above the `router.get('/:id', ...)` handler | Doc comment claims the handler fetches a user "by email address", but the handler parses `req.params.id` as an integer and matches on `u.id` — the doc comment contradicts the signature and behavior. | **Conflict** (doc comment doesn't match signature) |
| `evals/fixture/src/routes/users.ts` | Line 25 — the `// TODO: add request validation before creating user (reject empty name/email, enforce email format)` comment above the POST handler | An explicit `TODO` marker flagging missing request validation in an otherwise scoped area. | **Warning** (TODO/FIXME marker in scoped area) |

Together with the **Intentional Gap** above, these plants are the fixture's twin purposes: the missing health-check endpoint drives eval scenarios that ask agents to add new behavior, and the planted inconsistencies drive eval scenarios that ask agents to detect existing flaws.

## Usage

This fixture is **read by AI agents, not executed**. Do not run `npm install` in this directory. At eval time, the eval runner copies this directory to a temp location, deploys Smithy skills into the copy via `smithy init -a claude -y`, and runs `claude -p` against it.

To manually test Smithy skill deployment against a copy:

```bash
cp -r evals/fixture /tmp/fixture-test
node dist/cli.js init -a claude -y -d /tmp/fixture-test
# Inspect /tmp/fixture-test/.claude/ for deployed skills
rm -rf /tmp/fixture-test
```

## Adding Files

Only add files to this fixture when a specific eval scenario requires them. Keep the fixture minimal to reduce maintenance burden and ensure eval stability.

11	
12	Every artifact file begins with an H1 heading that follows this pattern:
13	`# <Type>: <Title>`
14	
15	| Artifact | H1 Format | Example |
16	|----------|-----------|---------|
17	| PRD | `# PRD: <Title>` | `# PRD: Prompt Template Linter` |
18	| RFC | `# RFC: <Title>` | `# RFC: Plugin System` |
19	| Feature Map | `# Feature Map: <Title>` | `# Feature Map: Core Pipeline` |
20	| Feature Spec | `# Feature Specification: <Title>` | `# Feature Specification: Webhook Support` |
21	| Data Model | `# Data Model: <Title>` | `# Data Model: Webhook Support` |
22	| Contracts | `# Contracts: <Title>` | `# Contracts: Webhook Support` |
23	| Tasks | `# Tasks: <Title>` | `# Tasks: Send Webhook on Build Complete` |
24	| Strike | `# Strike: <Title>` | `# Strike: Add Verbose Flag` |
25	
26	**Rules**:
27	- Always use a colon and single space after the type prefix: `<Type>: <Title>`.
28	- Title is in title case.
29	- The title should be concise — aim for 3-8 words.
30

34	
35	Sub-elements within artifacts use **numbered headings with colon separators**:
36	
37	| Sub-Element | Format | Example |
38	|-------------|--------|---------|
39	| Milestone | `### Milestone <N>: <Title>` | `### Milestone 1: Core Pipeline Commands` |
40	| Feature | `### Feature <N>: <Title>` | `### Feature 1: Template Deployment` |
41	| User Story | `### User Story <N>: <Title> (Priority: P<N>)` | `### User Story 1: Deploy Prompts (Priority: P1)` |
42	| Slice | `## Slice <N>: <Title>` | `## Slice 1: Add Template Reader` |
43	
44	**Rules**:
45	- All sub-elements use **colon** (`:`) as the separator between number and title.
46	- Numbers are sequential starting at 1 within their parent artifact.
47	- User stories include priority inline in parentheses after the title.
48	- Slice headings are H2 (`##`); all others are H3 (`###`).
49	
50	**Backward compatibility**: When **parsing** existing artifacts, also accept an
51	em dash (`—`) as the separator (e.g., `### User Story 1 — <Title>`). Older
52	specs may use this format. Always **write** new artifacts with the colon format.
53	
54	---
55	
56	## Slug & Folder Conventions
57	
58	Slugs are derived from titles using **kebab-case**: lowercase, hyphens for spaces,
59	alphanumeric characters only.
60	
61	| Artifact | Folder Pattern | Example |
62	|----------|----------------|---------|
63	| PRD | `docs/prds/<YYYY>-<NNN>-<slug>.prd.md` (flat file, no folder) | `docs/prds/2026-001-prompt-lint.prd.md` |
64	| RFC | `docs/rfcs/<YYYY>-<NNN>-<slug>/` | `docs/rfcs/2026-001-plugin-system/` |
65	| Feature Map | Co-located in RFC folder | `docs/rfcs/2026-001-plugin-system/01-core-pipeline.features.md` |
66	| Spec | `specs/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<NNN>-<slug>/` | `specs/2026-03-14-001-webhook-support/` |
67	| Tasks | Co-located in spec folder | `specs/.../03-send-webhook.tasks.md` |
68	| Strike | `specs/strikes/` | `specs/strikes/2026-03-14-verbose-flag.strike.md` |
69	
70	**Numbering**:
71	- `<NNN>` is zero-padded to 3 digits (001, 002, ...).
72	- Feature map and task file prefixes (`<NN>-`) are zero-padded to 2 digits (01, 02, ..., 99).
73	
74	---
75	
76	## Ticket Title Conventions
77	
78	When `smithy.orders` creates GitHub issues from artifacts, it uses **bracket
79	prefixes** to identify artifact type:
80	
81	| Artifact Source | Ticket Title Format |
82	|----------------|---------------------|
83	| RFC | `[RFC] <rfc-title>` |
84	| Milestone | `[RFC][Milestone] <milestone-title>` |
85	| Feature | `[Feature] <feature-title>` |
86	| User Story | `[Story] <story-title>` |
87	| Slice | `[Slice] <slice-title>` |
88	
89	**Rules**:
90	- Bracket prefixes are uppercase.
91	- The title after the brackets matches the artifact's title (not the slug).
92	- Do not include numbering in ticket titles (no "Feature 1:", just the title).
93	
94	---
95	
96	## Repo-Level Overrides
97	
98	Before writing artifact titles, check the project's **CLAUDE.md** (or equivalent
99	agent instructions file) for a section titled **"Smithy Title Conventions"** or
100	**"Title Conventions"**. If present, apply those overrides on top of the defaults
101	above.
102	
103	Common overrides include:
104	- **Title prefix**: e.g., `PROJ-123: RFC: <Title>` — prepend a ticket number or
105	  project code to all document titles.
106	- **Ticket prefix**: e.g., `[PROJ-123][RFC] <title>` — prepend a project code
107	  to all GitHub issue titles.
108	- **Custom type labels**: e.g., rename "Feature Specification" to "Feature Spec"
109	  in H1 headings.
110	- **Slug format**: e.g., require a team prefix in slug names.
111	
112	If no overrides section is found, use the defaults above as-is.

# smithy.titles

This prompt is the **single source of truth** for artifact title conventions
across all smithy planning commands. Every command that produces or parses
artifacts (spark, ignite, render, mark, cut, strike, orders) references this
prompt to ensure consistent naming.

---

## Document Title Conventions

Every artifact file begins with an H1 heading that follows this pattern:
`# <Type>: <Title>`

| Artifact | H1 Format | Example |
|----------|-----------|---------|
| PRD | `# PRD: <Title>` | `# PRD: Prompt Template Linter` |
| RFC | `# RFC: <Title>` | `# RFC: Plugin System` |
| Feature Map | `# Feature Map: <Title>` | `# Feature Map: Core Pipeline` |
| Feature Spec | `# Feature Specification: <Title>` | `# Feature Specification: Webhook Support` |
| Data Model | `# Data Model: <Title>` | `# Data Model: Webhook Support` |
| Contracts | `# Contracts: <Title>` | `# Contracts: Webhook Support` |
| Tasks | `# Tasks: <Title>` | `# Tasks: Send Webhook on Build Complete` |
| Strike | `# Strike: <Title>` | `# Strike: Add Verbose Flag` |

**Rules**:
- Always use a colon and single space after the type prefix: `<Type>: <Title>`.
- Title is in title case.
- The title should be concise — aim for 3-8 words.

---

## Sub-Element Title Conventions

Sub-elements within artifacts use **numbered headings with colon separators**:

| Sub-Element | Format | Example |
|-------------|--------|---------|
| Milestone | `### Milestone <N>: <Title>` | `### Milestone 1: Core Pipeline Commands` |
| Feature | `### Feature <N>: <Title>` | `### Feature 1: Template Deployment` |
| User Story | `### User Story <N>: <Title> (Priority: P<N>)` | `### User Story 1: Deploy Prompts (Priority: P1)` |
| Slice | `## Slice <N>: <Title>` | `## Slice 1: Add Template Reader` |

**Rules**:
- All sub-elements use **colon** (`:`) as the separator between number and title.
- Numbers are sequential starting at 1 within their parent artifact.
- User stories include priority inline in parentheses after the title.
- Slice headings are H2 (`##`); all others are H3 (`###`).

**Backward compatibility**: When **parsing** existing artifacts, also accept an
em dash (`—`) as the separator (e.g., `### User Story 1 — <Title>`). Older
specs may use this format. Always **write** new artifacts with the colon format.

---

## Slug & Folder Conventions

Slugs are derived from titles using **kebab-case**: lowercase, hyphens for spaces,
alphanumeric characters only.

| Artifact | Folder Pattern | Example |
|----------|----------------|---------|
| PRD | `docs/prds/<YYYY>-<NNN>-<slug>.prd.md` (flat file, no folder) | `docs/prds/2026-001-prompt-lint.prd.md` |
| RFC | `docs/rfcs/<YYYY>-<NNN>-<slug>/` | `docs/rfcs/2026-001-plugin-system/` |
| Feature Map | Co-located in RFC folder | `docs/rfcs/2026-001-plugin-system/01-core-pipeline.features.md` |
| Spec | `specs/<YYYY-MM-DD>-<NNN>-<slug>/` | `specs/2026-03-14-001-webhook-support/` |
| Tasks | Co-located in spec folder | `specs/.../03-send-webhook.tasks.md` |
| Strike | `specs/strikes/` | `specs/strikes/2026-03-14-verbose-flag.strike.md` |

**Numbering**:
- `<NNN>` is zero-padded to 3 digits (001, 002, ...).
- Feature map and task file prefixes (`<NN>-`) are zero-padded to 2 digits (01, 02, ..., 99).

---

## Ticket Title Conventions

When `smithy.orders` creates GitHub issues from artifacts, it uses **bracket
prefixes** to identify artifact type:

| Artifact Source | Ticket Title Format |
|----------------|---------------------|
| RFC | `[RFC] <rfc-title>` |
| Milestone | `[RFC][Milestone] <milestone-title>` |
| Feature | `[Feature] <feature-title>` |
| User Story | `[Story] <story-title>` |
| Slice | `[Slice] <slice-title>` |

**Rules**:
- Bracket prefixes are uppercase.
- The title after the brackets matches the artifact's title (not the slug).
- Do not include numbering in ticket titles (no "Feature 1:", just the title).

---

## Repo-Level Overrides

Before writing artifact titles, check the project's **CLAUDE.md** (or equivalent
agent instructions file) for a section titled **"Smithy Title Conventions"** or
**"Title Conventions"**. If present, apply those overrides on top of the defaults
above.

Common overrides include:
- **Title prefix**: e.g., `PROJ-123: RFC: <Title>` — prepend a ticket number or
  project code to all document titles.
- **Ticket prefix**: e.g., `[PROJ-123][RFC] <title>` — prepend a project code
  to all GitHub issue titles.
- **Custom type labels**: e.g., rename "Feature Specification" to "Feature Spec"
  in H1 headings.
- **Slug format**: e.g., require a team prefix in slug names.

If no overrides section is found, use the defaults above as-is.

You are designing the planning approach for a strike (lightweight, single-PR feature). Return a structured proposal with: Summary, Approach (file changes + rationale), Risks, Tradeoffs.

**Planning context**: strike document (single-PR scope, atomic commit, ~5 tasks max).

**Feature description**: Add a health check endpoint to a minimal Express TypeScript API.

**Codebase file paths** (already explored — these are the only relevant files):
- /tmp/smithy-eval-VeAPch/src/index.ts — Express app entry. Mounts `usersRouter` at `/api/users`, listens on `PORT`.
- /tmp/smithy-eval-VeAPch/src/routes/users.ts — Existing users Router with three handlers (list, get-by-id, create) — pattern reference for adding a new router.
- /tmp/smithy-eval-VeAPch/src/types.ts — Shared interfaces (`User`, `CreateUserRequest`).

1	# Smithy Eval Fixture
2	
3	This is a minimal Express TypeScript API project used as the **reference fixture** for Smithy eval scenarios. It is **not a real application** — it exists solely to give eval agents a concrete, stable codebase with specific file paths, route patterns, and data types that plans can reference.
4	
5	## Project Structure
6	
7	- `src/index.ts` — Express app entry point. Mounts the users router at `/api/users` and starts the server on a configurable `PORT`.
8	- `src/types.ts` — TypeScript interfaces (`User`, `CreateUserRequest`) used by route handlers.
9	- `src/routes/users.ts` — Express Router with three user CRUD handlers: list, get by id, and create.
10	- `package.json` — Node project manifest with Express as a runtime dependency, and TypeScript, `@types/express`, and `@types/node` as dev dependencies.
11	- `tsconfig.json` — TypeScript config targeting ES2022 with CommonJS module resolution.
12	
13	## Intentional Gap
14	
15	The app intentionally has **no health check endpoint**. This gap is the primary eval prompt target — eval scenarios ask agents to "add a health check endpoint" and validate that the resulting plan references the specific files and structures in this fixture.
16	
17	## Planted Inconsistencies
18	
19	In addition to the intentional gap above, the fixture contains **deliberate, scout-detectable inconsistencies** that exist solely to exercise smithy-scout coverage for **User Story 8 (US8)** of the evals framework spec (`specs/2026-04-06-003-smithy-evals-framework/08-fixture-deliberate-inconsistencies-for-scout.tasks.md`). **Do not "clean up" these plants.** Future maintainers, `smithy-fix`, and `smithy-scout` runs at deeper depths must leave them in place — removing them silently breaks the US8 scout scenario, which asserts that at least one Warning or Conflict finding is surfaced against this fixture.
20	
21	Each plant maps to a row in smithy-scout's Severity Guidelines table (see `src/templates/agent-skills/agents/smithy.scout.prompt`).
22	
23	| File | Location | Plant | Expected scout category |
24	|------|----------|-------|-------------------------|
25	| `evals/fixture/src/routes/users.ts` | Line 14 — the `// GET /:id — get user by email address` comment above the `router.get('/:id', ...)` handler | Doc comment claims the handler fetches a user "by email address", but the handler parses `req.params.id` as an integer and matches on `u.id` — the doc comment contradicts the signature and behavior. | **Conflict** (doc comment doesn't match signature) |
26	| `evals/fixture/src/routes/users.ts` | Line 25 — the `// TODO: add request validation before creating user (reject empty name/email, enforce email format)` comment above the POST handler | An explicit `TODO` marker flagging missing request validation in an otherwise scoped area. | **Warning** (TODO/FIXME marker in scoped area) |
27	
28	Together with the **Intentional Gap** above, these plants are the fixture's twin purposes: the missing health-check endpoint drives eval scenarios that ask agents to add new behavior, and the planted inconsistencies drive eval scenarios that ask agents to detect existing flaws.
29	
30	## Usage
31	
32	This fixture is **read by AI agents, not executed**. Do not run `npm install` in this directory. At eval time, the eval runner copies this directory to a temp location, deploys Smithy skills into the copy via `smithy init -a claude -y`, and runs `claude -p` against it.
33	
34	To manually test Smithy skill deployment against a copy:
35	
36	```bash
37	cp -r evals/fixture /tmp/fixture-test
38	node dist/cli.js init -a claude -y -d /tmp/fixture-test
39	# Inspect /tmp/fixture-test/.claude/ for deployed skills
40	rm -rf /tmp/fixture-test
41	```
42	
43	## Adding Files
44	
45	Only add files to this fixture when a specific eval scenario requires them. Keep the fixture minimal to reduce maintenance burden and ensure eval stability.
46

1	{
2	  "compilerOptions": {
3	    "target": "ES2022",
4	    "module": "commonjs",
5	    "strict": true,
6	    "esModuleInterop": true,
7	    "skipLibCheck": true,
8	    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,
9	    "rootDir": "src",
10	    "outDir": "dist"
11	  },
12	  "include": ["src"]
13	}
14

Reconcile these three competing strike-planning proposals into a single coherent plan. Preserve the strongest ideas from each, label any items unique to a single lens with `[via <lens>]`, and resolve conflicts with explicit rationale.

**Planning context**: strike document (single-PR scope, atomic commit, ~5 tasks max).

**Feature description**: Add a health check endpoint to a minimal Express TypeScript API.

**Codebase file paths**:
- /tmp/smithy-eval-VeAPch/src/index.ts
- /tmp/smithy-eval-VeAPch/src/routes/users.ts (do not modify — contains US8 plants)

- /tmp/smithy-eval-VeAPch/tsconfig.json
- /tmp/smithy-eval-VeAPch/README.md

---

**[Simplification]** Plan:

Summary: Add `GET /health` by inlining a single handler in `src/index.ts` returning `{ status: "ok" }`. No new files, no new types.

Approach:
- Edit only `src/index.ts` — add `app.get('/health', (_req, res) => res.json({ status: 'ok' }));` between `express.json()` and the users router mount.
- Mount at `/health` (root, not `/api/`), per orchestrator convention.
- No new types; literal response is fine.
- README "Intentional Gap" update is OPTIONAL — recommend skipping to preserve eval text.

Risks: README rephrasing may break eval grep; reviewer may want `/api/health` for symmetry; scope creep into readiness/version/uptime; accidental edits to users.ts plants.

Tradeoffs: Inline (recommended), separate Router (rejected — abstraction without payoff for one handler), `HealthResponse` interface (rejected — YAGNI), rich payload (rejected — speculative).

Final task count: 2 (edit index.ts, run tsc).

---

**[Separation of Concerns]** Plan:

Summary: Add `GET /api/health` via a new dedicated `src/routes/health.ts` Router mirroring `users.ts`, mounted in `index.ts`.

Approach:
1. New file `src/routes/health.ts` — default-export `Router` with `GET /` returning `{ status: 'ok', uptime: process.uptime(), timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }`.
2. Edit `src/index.ts` — `import healthRouter from './routes/health';` and `app.use('/api/health', healthRouter);` after the users mount.
3. Edit `src/types.ts` — add `HealthStatus` interface (status, uptime, timestamp). Optional but recommended.
4. Edit `README.md` — update "Intentional Gap" + "Project Structure"; leave Planted Inconsistencies table untouched.

Decisions: Mount at `/api/health` for consistency with `/api/users` (the fixture's only existing convention). Response includes uptime/timestamp because they're zero-dependency and demonstrate liveness (vs. cached 200).

Tradeoffs: Service-layer separation (`src/services/health.ts` + DI) — rejected, doesn't exist anywhere else in fixture; inline in index.ts — rejected as anti-directive; reuse users.ts — rejected as SRP violation; vitest setup — rejected (no runner installed).

Risks: Accidental modification of US8 plants; README edit accidentally rewriting Planted Inconsistencies; URL convention drift; future scope creep into dependency probes; non-determinism from process.uptime/Date complicating future tests.

Task count: 5.

---

**[Robustness]** Plan:

Summary: Add `GET /health` via new `src/routes/health.ts`, mount in `index.ts` at `/health` (top-level). Response: `{ status: 'ok' }` with HTTP 200. Liveness only — no readiness, no dependency checks (there are no dependencies).

Approach:
1. New file `src/routes/health.ts` — Router with `GET /` returning `res.status(200).json({ status: 'ok' })`. Use `_req: Request` to match users.ts:10.
2. Edit `src/index.ts` — import and `app.use('/health', healthRouter)` ABOVE the users mount.
3. Optional: `HealthResponse` interface in `src/types.ts`.
4. Optional: README update (parent agent should decide whether updating the gap text is in eval scope).

Decisions: Mount at `/health` for orchestrator convention; minimal `{status:'ok'}` payload because no dependencies; no try/catch (handler can't throw); no test runner.

Risks (defensive lens specifically called out):
- App-wide lack of error-handling middleware means any future throw bypasses JSON contract — flagged as out-of-scope concern.
- Liveness reports ok even if internal state corrupt — that's the definition of liveness; readiness is a separate concern.
- README update may break eval scenarios that grep the gap text.

Tradeoffs: Inline in index.ts (rejected — diverges from router pattern); /api/health mount (rejected — couples to API versioning); try/catch + JSON error middleware (favored by lens, rejected to keep scope atomic); rich payload with checks (rejected — no real dependencies); 405/406 method validation (rejected as overkill); zod request validation (rejected as out of scope).

Task count: 4.

---

**Reconciler instructions**:

Identify the conflicts and resolve them:
- File structure: inline in index.ts vs. new `src/routes/health.ts` Router file. Both Robustness and Separation prefer the Router file; Simplification prefers inline. Pattern-consistency with `users.ts` and the fixture's stated purpose ("give eval agents concrete, stable patterns to reference") favors the Router file. Note: Simplification's challenge is fair, but consistency with the existing pattern is itself a form of simplicity in a fixture whose stated purpose is to provide stable patterns.
- Mount path: `/api/health` (Separation) vs. `/health` (Simplification + Robustness). Two of three prefer `/health`. The orchestrator-convention argument is stronger than API-namespace symmetry, especially for a fixture that explicitly says it's not a real app.
- Response shape: `{status:'ok'}` (Simplification + Robustness) vs. `{status, uptime, timestamp}` (Separation). Two of three prefer minimal. Minimal aligns with fixture minimalism mandate; uptime/timestamp can be added later if needed.
- README update: optional everywhere; both Simplification and Robustness flag eval-grep risk. Default to leaving README untouched to preserve eval text; record as a Decision so the developer can override if they want.
- HealthResponse interface in types.ts: Separation recommends, Simplification rejects, Robustness optional. Default to skipping (matches the minimal payload — `{status:'ok'}` doesn't need a named type).
- Plant safety (US8): all three flag it. Out of Scope must explicitly include `src/routes/users.ts` and the README's Planted Inconsistencies table.

Return the reconciled plan as Summary, Approach (with file changes + rationale), Risks, Decisions, Tradeoffs, and Task list. Annotate items that came from a single lens with `[via <lens>]`.

1	# Smithy Eval Fixture
2	
3	This is a minimal Express TypeScript API project used as the **reference fixture** for Smithy eval scenarios. It is **not a real application** — it exists solely to give eval agents a concrete, stable codebase with specific file paths, route patterns, and data types that plans can reference.
4	
5	## Project Structure
6	
7	- `src/index.ts` — Express app entry point. Mounts the users router at `/api/users` and starts the server on a configurable `PORT`.
8	- `src/types.ts` — TypeScript interfaces (`User`, `CreateUserRequest`) used by route handlers.
9	- `src/routes/users.ts` — Express Router with three user CRUD handlers: list, get by id, and create.
10	- `package.json` — Node project manifest with Express as a runtime dependency, and TypeScript, `@types/express`, and `@types/node` as dev dependencies.
11	- `tsconfig.json` — TypeScript config targeting ES2022 with CommonJS module resolution.
12	
13	## Intentional Gap
14	
15	The app intentionally has **no health check endpoint**. This gap is the primary eval prompt target — eval scenarios ask agents to "add a health check endpoint" and validate that the resulting plan references the specific files and structures in this fixture.
16	
17	## Planted Inconsistencies
18	
19	In addition to the intentional gap above, the fixture contains **deliberate, scout-detectable inconsistencies** that exist solely to exercise smithy-scout coverage for **User Story 8 (US8)** of the evals framework spec (`specs/2026-04-06-003-smithy-evals-framework/08-fixture-deliberate-inconsistencies-for-scout.tasks.md`). **Do not "clean up" these plants.** Future maintainers, `smithy-fix`, and `smithy-scout` runs at deeper depths must leave them in place — removing them silently breaks the US8 scout scenario, which asserts that at least one Warning or Conflict finding is surfaced against this fixture.
20	
21	Each plant maps to a row in smithy-scout's Severity Guidelines table (see `src/templates/agent-skills/agents/smithy.scout.prompt`).
22	
23	| File | Location | Plant | Expected scout category |
24	|------|----------|-------|-------------------------|
25	| `evals/fixture/src/routes/users.ts` | Line 14 — the `// GET /:id — get user by email address` comment above the `router.get('/:id', ...)` handler | Doc comment claims the handler fetches a user "by email address", but the handler parses `req.params.id` as an integer and matches on `u.id` — the doc comment contradicts the signature and behavior. | **Conflict** (doc comment doesn't match signature) |
26	| `evals/fixture/src/routes/users.ts` | Line 25 — the `// TODO: add request validation before creating user (reject empty name/email, enforce email format)` comment above the POST handler | An explicit `TODO` marker flagging missing request validation in an otherwise scoped area. | **Warning** (TODO/FIXME marker in scoped area) |
27	
28	Together with the **Intentional Gap** above, these plants are the fixture's twin purposes: the missing health-check endpoint drives eval scenarios that ask agents to add new behavior, and the planted inconsistencies drive eval scenarios that ask agents to detect existing flaws.
29	
30	## Usage
31	
32	This fixture is **read by AI agents, not executed**. Do not run `npm install` in this directory. At eval time, the eval runner copies this directory to a temp location, deploys Smithy skills into the copy via `smithy init -a claude -y`, and runs `claude -p` against it.
33	
34	To manually test Smithy skill deployment against a copy:
35	
36	```bash
37	cp -r evals/fixture /tmp/fixture-test
38	node dist/cli.js init -a claude -y -d /tmp/fixture-test
39	# Inspect /tmp/fixture-test/.claude/ for deployed skills
40	rm -rf /tmp/fixture-test
41	```
42	
43	## Adding Files
44	
45	Only add files to this fixture when a specific eval scenario requires them. Keep the fixture minimal to reduce maintenance burden and ensure eval stability.
46

Code verified. The fixture's stated purpose ("give eval agents a concrete, stable codebase with specific file paths, route patterns, and data types that plans can reference") explicitly favors pattern-consistency. `users.ts` uses a default-exported `Router` with `_req: Request, res: Response` typing — that's the canonical pattern to mirror. Plants on lines 14 and 25 of `users.ts` are confirmed; README's Planted Inconsistencies table is on lines 17–28.

---

## Reconciled Plan

**Plans reconciled**: 3 (lenses: Simplification, Separation of Concerns, Robustness)
**Consensus items**: 5
**Unique finds**: 4
**Conflicts resolved**: 5

### Summary

Add a `GET /health` liveness endpoint by introducing a new `src/routes/health.ts` Router that mirrors the existing `users.ts` pattern, mounted at `/health` (top-level) in `src/index.ts`. Response is a minimal `{ status: 'ok' }` with HTTP 200. No new types, no README changes, no test scaffolding. The fixture's `src/routes/users.ts` US8 plants and the README's Planted Inconsistencies table are explicitly out of scope.

### Approach

**File changes:**

1. **Create `src/routes/health.ts`** (consensus: Separation + Robustness; rejected by Simplification — see Conflicts).
   - Default-export an Express `Router`.
   - Single handler: `router.get('/', (_req: Request, res: Response) => { res.status(200).json({ status: 'ok' }); });`.
   - Match `users.ts:10` typing exactly: `_req: Request, res: Response` imported from `'express'` [via Robustness — pattern-fidelity callout].
   - No try/catch (handler cannot throw) [via Robustness].

2. **Edit `src/index.ts`**.
   - Add `import healthRouter from './routes/health';` alongside the existing users import.
   - Add `app.use('/health', healthRouter);` **above** the `app.use('/api/users', ...)` mount [via Robustness — order chosen so liveness is registered before any feature router].
   - Mount path is `/health`, not `/api/health` (see Conflicts).

3. **Run `npx tsc` (or `npm run build`)** to verify the project still type-checks. No test runner is installed (`package.json` has no test script), so type-checking is the only available gate [consensus].

**Explicitly NOT changed:**
- `src/routes/users.ts` — contains US8 plants (line 14 doc-comment Conflict, line 25 TODO Warning); modifying this file silently breaks the US8 scout scenario [consensus].
- `src/types.ts` — no `HealthResponse` interface added; `{ status: 'ok' }` doesn't warrant a named type for a single-property literal (see Conflicts).
- `README.md` — Planted Inconsistencies table (lines 17–28) and "Intentional Gap" section (lines 13–15) left untouched to preserve eval grep targets (see Conflicts).

### Decisions

| Decision | Source | Alternatives | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-------------|-----------|
| Use a dedicated `src/routes/health.ts` Router file | consensus (Separation + Robustness) | Inline handler in `index.ts` [via Simplification] | Fixture's stated purpose is to provide stable patterns; mirroring `users.ts` is itself a form of simplicity here. |
| Mount at `/health` (top-level), not `/api/health` | consensus (Simplification + Robustness) | `/api/health` for symmetry with `/api/users` [via Separation] | Orchestrator convention; fixture is explicitly "not a real app" so API-namespace symmetry is weaker than convention. |
| Response shape `{ status: 'ok' }` only | consensus (Simplification + Robustness) | `{ status, uptime, timestamp }` [via Separation] | Aligns with fixture minimalism mandate ("Keep the fixture minimal", README line 45); uptime/timestamp can be added later without breaking callers. |
| Skip `HealthResponse` interface in `src/types.ts` | consensus (Simplification + Robustness-optional) | Add interface [via Separation] | Single-property literal doesn't warrant a named type; YAGNI. |
| Leave README untouched | consensus (Simplification + Robustness) | Update "Intentional Gap" + "Project Structure" [via Separation] | All three plans flag eval-grep risk; default to preservation. Developer can override if desired. |
| Place health mount above users mount in `index.ts` | [via Robustness] | After users mount [via Separation] | Liveness registered first is a defensible operational convention; either order works functionally. |
| HTTP 200 set explicitly via `res.status(200).json(...)` | [via Robustness] | `res.json(...)` (implicit 200) [via Simplification] | Explicit status documents the contract; trivial cost. |
| No error-handling middleware added | [via Robustness — flagged as out-of-scope concern] | Add app-wide JSON error middleware | Out of scope for an atomic single-PR strike; flagged for a future strike. |
| Skip test scaffolding | consensus | Add vitest [via Separation rejection] | No test runner in `package.json`; introducing one violates atomic-commit scope. |

### Conflicts

**Conflict 1: File structure — inline vs. dedicated Router file**
- **Option A** (Simplification): Inline `app.get('/health', ...)` in `src/index.ts`. Fewer files, fewest lines, zero abstraction overhead for a one-handler endpoint.
- **Option B** (Separation + Robustness): New `src/routes/health.ts` Router mirroring `users.ts`.
- **Recommendation**: Option B. Verified `users.ts` is the only routing pattern in the fixture, and the README explicitly states the fixture exists "to give eval agents a concrete, stable codebase with specific file paths, route patterns, and data types that plans can reference." Pattern-consistency is the dominant value here; Simplification's challenge is fair in a real app but inverted by the fixture's stated purpose.

**Conflict 2: Mount path — `/health` vs. `/api/health`**
- **Option A** (Simplification + Robustness): `/health` at top-level. Matches orchestrator/k8s liveness conventions.
- **Option B** (Separation): `/api/health` for symmetry with `/api/users`.
- **Recommendation**: Option A. Two of three lenses prefer it, and the fixture is explicitly "not a real application," weakening the API-namespace symmetry argument. Health is an operational concern, not an API resource.

**Conflict 3: Response shape — minimal vs. enriched**
- **Option A** (Simplification + Robustness): `{ status: 'ok' }`.
- **Option B** (Separation): `{ status, uptime: process.uptime(), timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }`.
- **Recommendation**: Option A. Fixture minimalism mandate (README line 45) dominates; uptime/timestamp introduce non-determinism that complicates any future test snapshot, as Separation itself flagged. Option B can be added incrementally without breaking the contract.

**Conflict 4: README update**
- **Option A** (Separation): Update "Intentional Gap" and "Project Structure" sections to reflect the new endpoint.
- **Option B** (Simplification + Robustness): Skip — eval scenarios may grep the gap text.
- **Recommendation**: Option B (skip). Both Simplification and Robustness independently flagged eval-grep risk, and the README explicitly says the gap text is "the primary eval prompt target." Surfaced as a Decision so the developer can override if they confirm no eval grep depends on the literal text.

**Conflict 5: `HealthResponse` interface in `src/types.ts`**
- **Option A** (Separation): Add interface for type discipline.
- **Option B** (Simplification): Reject as YAGNI. (Robustness: optional.)
- **Recommendation**: Option B (skip). A single-property string literal doesn't warrant a named type; the existing `User` and `CreateUserRequest` interfaces both have multiple fields and shared usage across handler + caller, which the health response does not.

### Risks

| Risk | Source | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|--------|-----------|------------|
| Accidental modification of `src/routes/users.ts` US8 plants (line 14 doc-comment, line 25 TODO) | consensus | Low if scope is respected; high if agent reflexively "cleans up" | Out-of-scope list explicitly names `src/routes/users.ts`; do not open it for edits during this strike. |
| Accidental edit to README's Planted Inconsistencies table or "Intentional Gap" text | consensus | Low | README left entirely untouched in this strike; if updated, table rows must be byte-identical. |
| Mount path drift (`/api/health` vs. `/health`) in future strikes | [via Separation] | Low | Decision recorded; future strikes should reference this decision before changing. |
| Eval grep on README "Intentional Gap" text breaks if README is updated | [via Simplification + Robustness] | Medium if README touched | Default to no README change; document as overridable Decision. |
| App lacks global error-handling middleware — any future router throw bypasses JSON contract | [via Robustness] | N/A for this strike (handler cannot throw) | Flagged for a future strike; out of scope here. |
| Liveness reports `ok` even if internal state is corrupt | [via Robustness] | By design | That is the definition of liveness; readiness is a separate concern out of scope. |
| Scope creep into readiness/version/uptime/dependency probes | consensus | Medium | Atomic commit; reject enrichment in code review. |
| Non-determinism from `process.uptime()` / `new Date()` complicating future tests | [via Separation — self-flagged] | N/A given chosen minimal payload | Avoided by choosing `{ status: 'ok' }`. |

### Tradeoffs

| Alternative | Favored by | Pros | Cons |
|------------|-----------|------|------|
| Inline handler in `index.ts` | Simplification | 2 tasks, zero new files, lowest diff | Diverges from established Router pattern; fixture's stated purpose is pattern stability. |
| Dedicated Router file (chosen) | Separation, Robustness | Mirrors `users.ts`; reinforces the pattern eval agents reference | One more file; trivial overhead for a one-handler endpoint. |
| Mount at `/api/health` | Separation | Symmetric with `/api/users` | Couples liveness to API versioning; not orchestrator-standard. |
| Mount at `/health` (chosen) | Simplification, Robustness | Orchestrator-standard; decoupled from API namespace | Asymmetric with `/api/users`. |
| Rich `{status, uptime, timestamp}` payload | Separation | Demonstrates liveness vs. cached 200 | Non-deterministic; future test friction; YAGNI for fixture. |
| Service-layer separation (`src/services/health.ts` + DI) | (rejected by Separation) | Industrial-strength | No service layer exists anywhere in fixture; massive overkill. |
| Add `HealthResponse` to `types.ts` | Separation | Type discipline | YAGNI for `{ status: 'ok' }`. |
| Update README "Intentional Gap" + "Project Structure" | Separation | Docs stay accurate | Risks breaking eval grep targets. |
| Add try/catch + JSON error middleware | (favored by Robustness lens, rejected for scope) | Defensive; future-proofs JSON contract | Violates atomic single-PR scope; deserves its own strike. |
| Add 405/406 method validation | (rejected by Robustness) | Stricter HTTP correctness | Overkill for liveness. |
| Add zod request validation | (rejected by Robustness) | Input safety | No request body on GET /health. |
| Add vitest test suite | (rejected by Separation) | Regression coverage | No test runner installed; introducing one breaks atomic scope. |

### Task List (5 max)

1. **Create `src/routes/health.ts`** — Default-export a `Router` with `GET /` returning `res.status(200).json({ status: 'ok' })`. Use `_req: Request, res: Response` imports from `'express'` to mirror `users.ts:10`.
2. **Edit `src/index.ts`** — Add `import healthRouter from './routes/health';` and `app.use('/health', healthRouter);` immediately before the existing `app.use('/api/users', usersRouter);` line.
3. **Type-check** — Run `npx tsc --noEmit` (or `npm run build`) to confirm no TypeScript errors. No test runner is installed, so this is the available correctness gate.
4. **Manual smoke verification** — Mentally trace (or, if running locally, `curl localhost:3000/health`) to confirm the route returns `{"status":"ok"}` with HTTP 200 and that `/api/users` is unaffected.
5. **Out-of-scope guard check** — Confirm the diff touches only `src/index.ts` (modified) and `src/routes/health.ts` (new). Confirm `src/routes/users.ts`, `src/types.ts`, `README.md`, `package.json`, and `tsconfig.json` are unchanged.

---

- `/tmp/smithy-eval-VeAPch/src/index.ts` (modify — add import + mount)
- `/tmp/smithy-eval-VeAPch/src/routes/health.ts` (create)
- `/tmp/smithy-eval-VeAPch/src/routes/users.ts` (out of scope — US8 plants on lines 14, 25)

- /tmp/smithy-eval-VeAPch/src/types.ts
- /tmp/smithy-eval-VeAPch/package.json (no test runner installed)
- /tmp/smithy-eval-VeAPch/tsconfig.json
- /tmp/smithy-eval-VeAPch/README.md

Summary: Add `GET /health` by inlining a single handler in `src/index.ts` returning `{ status: "ok" }`. No new files, no new types.

Approach:
- Edit only `src/index.ts` — add `app.get('/health', (_req, res) => res.json({ status: 'ok' }));` between `express.json()` and the users router mount.
- Mount at `/health` (root, not `/api/`), per orchestrator convention.
- No new types; literal response is fine.
- README "Intentional Gap" update is OPTIONAL — recommend skipping to preserve eval text.

Risks: README rephrasing may break eval grep; reviewer may want `/api/health` for symmetry; scope creep into readiness/version/uptime; accidental edits to users.ts plants.

Tradeoffs: Inline (recommended), separate Router (rejected — abstraction without payoff for one handler), `HealthResponse` interface (rejected — YAGNI), rich payload (rejected — speculative).

Final task count: 2 (edit index.ts, run tsc).

**[Separation of Concerns]** Plan:

Summary: Add `GET /api/health` via a new dedicated `src/routes/health.ts` Router mirroring `users.ts`, mounted in `index.ts`.

Approach:
1. New file `src/routes/health.ts` — default-export `Router` with `GET /` returning `{ status: 'ok', uptime: process.uptime(), timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }`.
2. Edit `src/index.ts` — `import healthRouter from './routes/health';` and `app.use('/api/health', healthRouter);` after the users mount.
3. Edit `src/types.ts` — add `HealthStatus` interface (status, uptime, timestamp). Optional but recommended.
4. Edit `README.md` — update "Intentional Gap" + "Project Structure"; leave Planted Inconsistencies table untouched.

Decisions: Mount at `/api/health` for consistency with `/api/users` (the fixture's only existing convention). Response includes uptime/timestamp because they're zero-dependency and demonstrate liveness (vs. cached 200).

Tradeoffs: Service-layer separation (`src/services/health.ts` + DI) — rejected, doesn't exist anywhere else in fixture; inline in index.ts — rejected as anti-directive; reuse users.ts — rejected as SRP violation; vitest setup — rejected (no runner installed).

Code verified. The fixture's stated purpose ("give eval agents a concrete, stable codebase with specific file paths, route patterns, and data types that plans can reference") explicitly favors pattern-consistency. `users.ts` uses a default-exported `Router` with `_req: Request, res: Response` typing — that's the canonical pattern to mirror. Plants on lines 14 and 25 of `users.ts` are confirmed; README's Planted Inconsistencies table is on lines 17–28.

---

## Reconciled Plan

**Plans reconciled**: 3 (lenses: Simplification, Separation of Concerns, Robustness)
**Consensus items**: 5
**Unique finds**: 4
**Conflicts resolved**: 5

### Summary

Add a `GET /health` liveness endpoint by introducing a new `src/routes/health.ts` Router that mirrors the existing `users.ts` pattern, mounted at `/health` (top-level) in `src/index.ts`. Response is a minimal `{ status: 'ok' }` with HTTP 200. No new types, no README changes, no test scaffolding. The fixture's `src/routes/users.ts` US8 plants and the README's Planted Inconsistencies table are explicitly out of scope.

### Approach

**File changes:**

1. **Create `src/routes/health.ts`** (consensus: Separation + Robustness; rejected by Simplification — see Conflicts).
   - Default-export an Express `Router`.
   - Single handler: `router.get('/', (_req: Request, res: Response) => { res.status(200).json({ status: 'ok' }); });`.
   - Match `users.ts:10` typing exactly: `_req: Request, res: Response` imported from `'express'` [via Robustness — pattern-fidelity callout].
   - No try/catch (handler cannot throw) [via Robustness].

2. **Edit `src/index.ts`**.
   - Add `import healthRouter from './routes/health';` alongside the existing users import.
   - Add `app.use('/health', healthRouter);` **above** the `app.use('/api/users', ...)` mount [via Robustness — order chosen so liveness is registered before any feature router].
   - Mount path is `/health`, not `/api/health` (see Conflicts).

3. **Run `npx tsc` (or `npm run build`)** to verify the project still type-checks. No test runner is installed (`package.json` has no test script), so type-checking is the only available gate [consensus].

**Explicitly NOT changed:**
- `src/routes/users.ts` — contains US8 plants (line 14 doc-comment Conflict, line 25 TODO Warning); modifying this file silently breaks the US8 scout scenario [consensus].
- `src/types.ts` — no `HealthResponse` interface added; `{ status: 'ok' }` doesn't warrant a named type for a single-property literal (see Conflicts).
- `README.md` — Planted Inconsistencies table (lines 17–28) and "Intentional Gap" section (lines 13–15) left untouched to preserve eval grep targets (see Conflicts).

### Decisions

| Decision | Source | Alternatives | Rationale |
|----------|--------|-------------|-----------|
| Use a dedicated `src/routes/health.ts` Router file | consensus (Separation + Robustness) | Inline handler in `index.ts` [via Simplification] | Fixture's stated purpose is to provide stable patterns; mirroring `users.ts` is itself a form of simplicity here. |
| Mount at `/health` (top-level), not `/api/health` | consensus (Simplification + Robustness) | `/api/health` for symmetry with `/api/users` [via Separation] | Orchestrator convention; fixture is explicitly "not a real app" so API-namespace symmetry is weaker than convention. |
| Response shape `{ status: 'ok' }` only | consensus (Simplification + Robustness) | `{ status, uptime, timestamp }` [via Separation] | Aligns with fixture minimalism mandate ("Keep the fixture minimal", README line 45); uptime/timestamp can be added later without breaking callers. |
| Skip `HealthResponse` interface in `src/types.ts` | consensus (Simplification + Robustness-optional) | Add interface [via Separation] | Single-property literal doesn't warrant a named type; YAGNI. |
| Leave README untouched | consensus (Simplification + Robustness) | Update "Intentional Gap" + "Project Structure" [via Separation] | All three plans flag eval-grep risk; default to preservation. Developer can override if desired. |
| Place health mount above users mount in `index.ts` | [via Robustness] | After users mount [via Separation] | Liveness registered first is a defensible operational convention; either order works functionally. |
| HTTP 200 set explicitly via `res.status(200).json(...)` | [via Robustness] | `res.json(...)` (implicit 200) [via Simplification] | Explicit status documents the contract; trivial cost. |
| No error-handling middleware added | [via Robustness — flagged as out-of-scope concern] | Add app-wide JSON error middleware | Out of scope for an atomic single-PR strike; flagged for a future strike. |
| Skip test scaffolding | consensus | Add vitest [via Separation rejection] | No test runner in `package.json`; introducing one violates atomic-commit scope. |

### Conflicts

**Conflict 1: File structure — inline vs. dedicated Router file**
- **Option A** (Simplification): Inline `app.get('/health', ...)` in `src/index.ts`. Fewer files, fewest lines, zero abstraction overhead for a one-handler endpoint.
- **Option B** (Separation + Robustness): New `src/routes/health.ts` Router mirroring `users.ts`.
- **Recommendation**: Option B. Verified `users.ts` is the only routing pattern in the fixture, and the README explicitly states the fixture exists "to give eval agents a concrete, stable codebase with specific file paths, route patterns, and data types that plans can reference." Pattern-consistency is the dominant value here; Simplification's challenge is fair in a real app but inverted by the fixture's stated purpose.

**Conflict 2: Mount path — `/health` vs. `/api/health`**
- **Option A** (Simplification + Robustness): `/health` at top-level. Matches orchestrator/k8s liveness conventions.
- **Option B** (Separation): `/api/health` for symmetry with `/api/users`.
- **Recommendation**: Option A. Two of three lenses prefer it, and the fixture is explicitly "not a real application," weakening the API-namespace symmetry argument. Health is an operational concern, not an API resource.

**Conflict 3: Response shape — minimal vs. enriched**
- **Option A** (Simplification + Robustness): `{ status: 'ok' }`.
- **Option B** (Separation): `{ status, uptime: process.uptime(), timestamp: new Date().toISOString() }`.
- **Recommendation**: Option A. Fixture minimalism mandate (README line 45) dominates; uptime/timestamp introduce non-determinism that complicates any future test snapshot, as Separation itself flagged. Option B can be added incrementally without breaking the contract.

**Conflict 4: README update**
- **Option A** (Separation): Update "Intentional Gap" and "Project Structure" sections to reflect the new endpoint.
- **Option B** (Simplification + Robustness): Skip — eval scenarios may grep the gap text.
- **Recommendation**: Option B (skip). Both Simplification and Robustness independently flagged eval-grep risk, and the README explicitly says the gap text is "the primary eval prompt target." Surfaced as a Decision so the developer can override if they confirm no eval grep depends on the literal text.

**Conflict 5: `HealthResponse` interface in `src/types.ts`**
- **Option A** (Separation): Add interface for type discipline.
- **Option B** (Simplification): Reject as YAGNI. (Robustness: optional.)
- **Recommendation**: Option B (skip). A single-property string literal doesn't warrant a named type; the existing `User` and `CreateUserRequest` interfaces both have multiple fields and shared usage across handler + caller, which the health response does not.

### Risks

| Risk | Source | Likelihood | Mitigation |
|------|--------|-----------|------------|
| Accidental modification of `src/routes/users.ts` US8 plants (line 14 doc-comment, line 25 TODO) | consensus | Low if scope is respected; high if agent reflexively "cleans up" | Out-of-scope list explicitly names `src/routes/users.ts`; do not open it for edits during this strike. |
| Accidental edit to README's Planted Inconsistencies table or "Intentional Gap" text | consensus | Low | README left entirely untouched in this strike; if updated, table rows must be byte-identical. |
| Mount path drift (`/api/health` vs. `/health`) in future strikes | [via Separation] | Low | Decision recorded; future strikes should reference this decision before changing. |
| Eval grep on README "Intentional Gap" text breaks if README is updated | [via Simplification + Robustness] | Medium if README touched | Default to no README change; document as overridable Decision. |
| App lacks global error-handling middleware — any future router throw bypasses JSON contract | [via Robustness] | N/A for this strike (handler cannot throw) | Flagged for a future strike; out of scope here. |
| Liveness reports `ok` even if internal state is corrupt | [via Robustness] | By design | That is the definition of liveness; readiness is a separate concern out of scope. |
| Scope creep into readiness/version/uptime/dependency probes | consensus | Medium | Atomic commit; reject enrichment in code review. |
| Non-determinism from `process.uptime()` / `new Date()` complicating future tests | [via Separation — self-flagged] | N/A given chosen minimal payload | Avoided by choosing `{ status: 'ok' }`. |

### Tradeoffs

| Alternative | Favored by | Pros | Cons |
|------------|-----------|------|------|
| Inline handler in `index.ts` | Simplification | 2 tasks, zero new files, lowest diff | Diverges from established Router pattern; fixture's stated purpose is pattern stability. |
| Dedicated Router file (chosen) | Separation, Robustness | Mirrors `users.ts`; reinforces the pattern eval agents reference | One more file; trivial overhead for a one-handler endpoint. |
| Mount at `/api/health` | Separation | Symmetric with `/api/users` | Couples liveness to API versioning; not orchestrator-standard. |
| Mount at `/health` (chosen) | Simplification, Robustness | Orchestrator-standard; decoupled from API namespace | Asymmetric with `/api/users`. |
| Rich `{status, uptime, timestamp}` payload | Separation | Demonstrates liveness vs. cached 200 | Non-deterministic; future test friction; YAGNI for fixture. |
| Service-layer separation (`src/services/health.ts` + DI) | (rejected by Separation) | Industrial-strength | No service layer exists anywhere in fixture; massive overkill. |
| Add `HealthResponse` to `types.ts` | Separation | Type discipline | YAGNI for `{ status: 'ok' }`. |
| Update README "Intentional Gap" + "Project Structure" | Separation | Docs stay accurate | Risks breaking eval grep targets. |
| Add try/catch + JSON error middleware | (favored by Robustness lens, rejected for scope) | Defensive; future-proofs JSON contract | Violates atomic single-PR scope; deserves its own strike. |
| Add 405/406 method validation | (rejected by Robustness) | Stricter HTTP correctness | Overkill for liveness. |
| Add zod request validation | (rejected by Robustness) | Input safety | No request body on GET /health. |
| Add vitest test suite | (rejected by Separation) | Regression coverage | No test runner installed; introducing one breaks atomic scope. |

### Task List (5 max)

1. **Create `src/routes/health.ts`** — Default-export a `Router` with `GET /` returning `res.status(200).json({ status: 'ok' })`. Use `_req: Request, res: Response` imports from `'express'` to mirror `users.ts:10`.
2. **Edit `src/index.ts`** — Add `import healthRouter from './routes/health';` and `app.use('/health', healthRouter);` immediately before the existing `app.use('/api/users', usersRouter);` line.
3. **Type-check** — Run `npx tsc --noEmit` (or `npm run build`) to confirm no TypeScript errors. No test runner is installed, so this is the available correctness gate.
4. **Manual smoke verification** — Mentally trace (or, if running locally, `curl localhost:3000/health`) to confirm the route returns `{"status":"ok"}` with HTTP 200 and that `/api/users` is unaffected.
5. **Out-of-scope guard check** — Confirm the diff touches only `src/index.ts` (modified) and `src/routes/health.ts` (new). Confirm `src/routes/users.ts`, `src/types.ts`, `README.md`, `package.json`, and `tsconfig.json` are unchanged.

---

- `/tmp/smithy-eval-VeAPch/src/index.ts` (modify — add import + mount)
- `/tmp/smithy-eval-VeAPch/src/routes/health.ts` (create)

input.content:
# Strike: Add Health Check Endpoint

**Date:** 2026-04-27  |  **Branch:** strike/health-check-endpoint  |  **Status:** Ready

## Summary

Add a `GET /health` liveness endpoint to the minimal Express TypeScript API
fixture. The endpoint is implemented as a new dedicated `Router` in
`src/routes/health.ts` (mirroring the existing `src/routes/users.ts`
pattern) and mounted at the top-level `/health` path in `src/index.ts`.
The handler responds with HTTP 200 and a minimal JSON body
`{ "status": "ok" }`. The fixture's `users.ts` US8 plants and the
`README.md` Planted Inconsistencies / Intentional Gap text are explicitly
left untouched.

## Goal

A single `GET /health` endpoint returns `200 {"status":"ok"}` so that
external probes (orchestrators, load balancers, manual `curl`) can confirm
the Express process is alive.

## Out of Scope

- `src/routes/users.ts` — contains US8 scout plants on lines 14 and 25; do
  not modify.
- `README.md` — Intentional Gap section (lines 13–15) and Planted
  Inconsistencies table (lines 17–28) must remain byte-identical to
  preserve eval grep targets.
- `src/types.ts` — no `HealthResponse` interface added (single-property
  literal does not warrant a named type).
- Readiness/dependency probes (no DB, cache, or downstream services exist
  in the fixture).
- App-wide JSON error-handling middleware (real gap, but a separate strike).
- Test scaffolding (`package.json` has no test runner; introducing one
  violates the atomic-commit budget).
- Response enrichment with `uptime`, `timestamp`, or `version`.
- Mounting under `/api/` (rejected — health is operational, not API).

## Requirements

- **FR-001**: Expose `GET /health` returning HTTP 200 with JSON body
  `{ "status": "ok" }` and Content-Type `application/json`.
- **FR-002**: Implement the handler in a new file `src/routes/health.ts`
  that default-exports an Express `Router`, mirroring the structure and
  typing conventions of `src/routes/users.ts` (default export, `Router`
  imported from `'express'`, `_req: Request, res: Response` signature).
- **FR-003**: Mount the router in `src/index.ts` via
  `app.use('/health', healthRouter)`, placed immediately above the
  existing `app.use('/api/users', usersRouter)` line.
- **FR-004**: Set HTTP status explicitly: `res.status(200).json({ status: 'ok' })`.
- **FR-005**: Existing `/api/users` routes (list, get-by-id, create) must
  continue to function unchanged.

## Success Criteria

- **SC-001**: `npx tsc --noEmit` (or `npm run build`) completes without
  errors after the change.
- **SC-002**: `curl -i http://localhost:$PORT/health` returns HTTP 200
  with header `Content-Type: application/json` and body
  `{"status":"ok"}`.
- **SC-003**: `curl http://localhost:$PORT/api/users` continues to return
  the existing JSON list response (regression check).
- **SC-004**: The diff touches exactly two files: `src/routes/health.ts`
  (new) and `src/index.ts` (modified). `src/routes/users.ts`,
  `src/types.ts`, `README.md`, `package.json`, and `tsconfig.json` are
  unchanged.

## User Flow

An operator or orchestrator issues `GET /health` against the running
server. The server responds immediately with HTTP 200 and the JSON body
`{"status":"ok"}`. The probe interprets any 2xx response as "process is
alive" and any non-2xx (or no response) as "unhealthy/unreachable." The
endpoint requires no authentication, accepts no query parameters, and
performs no I/O.

## Data Model

N/A — the endpoint reads no persistent state and writes no data. The
response body is a fixed JSON literal.

## Contracts

A single new HTTP contract is added:

| Method | Path     | Request body | Response status | Response body         | Content-Type     |
|--------|----------|--------------|-----------------|-----------------------|------------------|
| GET    | /health  | none         | 200             | `{"status":"ok"}`     | application/json |

No changes to the existing `/api/users` contracts.

## Decisions

- **Dedicated Router file (vs inline in `index.ts`)** — chosen to mirror
  the established `src/routes/users.ts` pattern. The Simplification lens
  argued for inlining; rejected because the fixture's stated purpose
  (per README) is to provide stable patterns for eval agents to
  reference. Pattern fidelity is itself a form of simplicity here.
- **Mount path `/health` (vs `/api/health`)** — chosen for orchestrator
  convention. The Separation lens argued for `/api/health` for symmetry
  with `/api/users`; rejected because health is an operational concern,
  not a versioned API resource, and the fixture is explicitly "not a
  real application."
- **Minimal `{status:'ok'}` payload (vs `{status, uptime, timestamp}`)**
  — chosen to match fixture minimalism. Uptime/timestamp can be added
  later without breaking callers and would introduce non-determinism
  that complicates any future test snapshot.
- **Skip `HealthResponse` interface in `src/types.ts`** — chosen as YAGNI.
  The existing `User`/`CreateUserRequest` interfaces have multiple fields
  and shared cross-handler usage; a single-property string literal does
  not warrant a named type.
- **Leave `README.md` untouched** — chosen to preserve eval grep targets.
  The README explicitly identifies the missing health check as the eval
  prompt target; rephrasing the "Intentional Gap" stanza risks breaking
  scenarios that grep that text.
- **Explicit `res.status(200)`** — chosen over implicit `res.json(...)`
  to document the contract at the call site. Trivial cost.
- **No try/catch and no app-wide error middleware** — the handler cannot
  throw (no I/O, no computation). The fixture's app-wide lack of JSON
  error middleware is a real defensive gap flagged by the Robustness
  lens, but fixing it belongs in a separate strike.
- **Skip test scaffolding** — `package.json` has no test runner;
  introducing vitest/jest/supertest would balloon the strike past the
  ~5-task budget and contradict the fixture's "intentionally minimal"
  mandate. Type-checking plus manual smoke is the available gate.

## Specification Debt

| ID | Description | Source Category | Impact | Confidence | Status | Resolution |
|----|-------------|-----------------|--------|------------|--------|------------|
| SD-001 | Error response shape for `/health` is unspecified. The strike defines only the 200 path; non-2xx responses would fall to Express's default HTML error page, inconsistent with `users.ts`'s `{error: '...'}` JSON convention. Likely acceptable for a liveness probe (any non-2xx = unhealthy) but not explicitly acknowledged in the reconciled decisions. | Edge Cases | Low | Medium | open | — |

## Single Slice

**Goal**: Deliver a working `GET /health` liveness endpoint in one
atomic commit that introduces a new router file, mounts it in the entry
point, and leaves all out-of-scope files byte-identical.

**Justification**: The change is a self-contained, single-resource
addition with no migrations, no data model changes, no contract evolution,
and no cross-cutting concerns. It can be verified end-to-end with a
single type-check pass and a single `curl` smoke test. Splitting it
further would create churn without isolating risk.

### Tasks

- [ ] Task 1: Create `src/routes/health.ts` — default-export an Express
  `Router` with one handler:
  `router.get('/', (_req: Request, res: Response) => { res.status(200).json({ status: 'ok' }); });`.
  Imports: `Router, Request, Response` from `'express'`. Mirror the
  formatting of `src/routes/users.ts` (whitespace, comment style above
  the handler, default export at the bottom).
- [ ] Task 2: Edit `src/index.ts` — add
  `import healthRouter from './routes/health';` alongside the existing
  users import, and add `app.use('/health', healthRouter);` immediately
  above the existing `app.use('/api/users', usersRouter);` line.
- [ ] Task 3: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` (or `npm run build`) and confirm
  zero TypeScript errors.
- [ ] Task 4: Manual smoke verification — start the server (or mentally
  trace if execution is unavailable) and confirm
  `curl -i http://localhost:$PORT/health` returns
  `HTTP/1.1 200 OK`, `Content-Type: application/json`, body
  `{"status":"ok"}`; confirm `GET /api/users` still returns its existing
  response.
- [ ] Task 5: Out-of-scope guard — run `git diff --stat` and confirm only
  `src/routes/health.ts` (new) and `src/index.ts` (modified) appear in
  the diff. `src/routes/users.ts`, `src/types.ts`, `README.md`,
  `package.json`, and `tsconfig.json` must be unchanged.

**PR Outcome**: Merging the PR adds a `GET /health` endpoint to the
fixture's running Express server, returning `200 {"status":"ok"}`. The
fixture's stated intentional gap (per README) is filled at the code
level while leaving the documented gap text and US8 plants untouched, so
existing eval scenarios that depend on either the missing-endpoint code
state or the planted scout-detectable inconsistencies continue to behave
as before.

| Method | Path     | Request body | Response status | Response body         | Content-Type     |
|--------|----------|--------------|-----------------|-----------------------|------------------|
| GET    | /health  | none         | 200             | `{"status":"ok"}`     | application/json |

No changes to the existing `/api/users` contracts.

## Decisions

- **Dedicated Router file (vs inline in `index.ts`)** — chosen to mirror
  the established `src/routes/users.ts` pattern. The Simplification lens
  argued for inlining; rejected because the fixture's stated purpose
  (per README) is to provide stable patterns for eval agents to
  reference. Pattern fidelity is itself a form of simplicity here.
- **Mount path `/health` (vs `/api/health`)** — chosen for orchestrator
  convention. The Separation lens argued for `/api/health` for symmetry
  with `/api/users`; rejected because health is an operational concern,
  not a versioned API resource, and the fixture is explicitly "not a
  real application."
- **Minimal `{status:'ok'}` payload (vs `{status, uptime, timestamp}`)**
  — chosen to match fixture minimalism. Uptime/timestamp can be added
  later without breaking callers and would introduce non-determinism
  that complicates any future test snapshot.
- **Skip `HealthResponse` interface in `src/types.ts`** — chosen as YAGNI.
  The existing `User`/`CreateUserRequest` interfaces have multiple fields
  and shared cross-handler usage; a single-property string literal does
  not warrant a named type.
- **Leave `README.md` untouched** — chosen to preserve eval grep targets.
  The README explicitly identifies the missing health check as the eval
  prompt target; rephrasing the "Intentional Gap" stanza risks breaking
  scenarios that grep that text.
- **Explicit `res.status(200)`** — chosen over implicit `res.json(...)`
  to document the contract at the call site. Trivial cost.
- **No try/catch and no app-wide error middleware** — the handler cannot
  throw (no I/O, no computation). The fixture's app-wide lack of JSON
  error middleware is a real defensive gap flagged by the Robustness
  lens, but fixing it belongs in a separate strike.
- **Skip test scaffolding** — `package.json` has no test runner;
  introducing vitest/jest/supertest would balloon the strike past the
  ~5-task budget and contradict the fixture's "intentionally minimal"
  mandate. Type-checking plus manual smoke is the available gate.

## Specification Debt

| ID | Description | Source Category | Impact | Confidence | Status | Resolution |
|----|-------------|-----------------|--------|------------|--------|------------|
| SD-001 | Error response shape for `/health` is unspecified. The strike defines only the 200 path; non-2xx responses would fall to Express's default HTML error page, inconsistent with `users.ts`'s `{error: '...'}` JSON convention. Likely acceptable for a liveness probe (any non-2xx = unhealthy) but not explicitly acknowledged in the reconciled decisions. | Edge Cases | Low | Medium | open | — |

## Single Slice

**Goal**: Deliver a working `GET /health` liveness endpoint in one
atomic commit that introduces a new router file, mounts it in the entry
point, and leaves all out-of-scope files byte-identical.

**Justification**: The change is a self-contained, single-resource
addition with no migrations, no data model changes, no contract evolution,
and no cross-cutting concerns. It can be verified end-to-end with a
single type-check pass and a single `curl` smoke test. Splitting it
further would create churn without isolating risk.

### Tasks

- [ ] Task 1: Create `src/routes/health.ts` — default-export an Express
  `Router` with one handler:
  `router.get('/', (_req: Request, res: Response) => { res.status(200).json({ status: 'ok' }); });`.
  Imports: `Router, Request, Response` from `'express'`. Mirror the
  formatting of `src/routes/users.ts` (whitespace, comment style above
  the handler, default export at the bottom).
- [ ] Task 2: Edit `src/index.ts` — add
  `import healthRouter from './routes/health';` alongside the existing
  users import, and add `app.use('/health', healthRouter);` immediately
  above the existing `app.use('/api/users', usersRouter);` line.
- [ ] Task 3: Run `npx tsc --noEmit` (or `npm run build`) and confirm
  zero TypeScript errors.
- [ ] Task 4: Manual smoke verification — start the server (or mentally
  trace if execution is unavailable) and confirm
  `curl -i http://localhost:$PORT/health` returns
  `HTTP/1.1 200 OK`, `Content-Type: application/json`, body
  `{"status":"ok"}`; confirm `GET /api/users` still returns its existing
  response.
- [ ] Task 5: Out-of-scope guard — run `git diff --stat` and confirm only
  `src/routes/health.ts` (new) and `src/index.ts` (modified) appear in
  the diff. `src/routes/users.ts`, `src/types.ts`, `README.md`,
  `package.json`, and `tsconfig.json` must be unchanged.

**PR Outcome**: Merging the PR adds a `GET /health` endpoint to the
fixture's running Express server, returning `200 {"status":"ok"}`. The
fixture's stated intentional gap (per README) is filled at the code
level while leaving the documented gap text and US8 plants untouched, so
existing eval scenarios that depend on either the missing-endpoint code
state or the planted scout-detectable inconsistencies continue to behave
as before.

1	# Smithy Eval Fixture
2	
3	This is a minimal Express TypeScript API project used as the **reference fixture** for Smithy eval scenarios. It is **not a real application** — it exists solely to give eval agents a concrete, stable codebase with specific file paths, route patterns, and data types that plans can reference.
4	
5	## Project Structure
6	
7	- `src/index.ts` — Express app entry point. Mounts the users router at `/api/users` and starts the server on a configurable `PORT`.
8	- `src/types.ts` — TypeScript interfaces (`User`, `CreateUserRequest`) used by route handlers.
9	- `src/routes/users.ts` — Express Router with three user CRUD handlers: list, get by id, and create.
10	- `package.json` — Node project manifest with Express as a runtime dependency, and TypeScript, `@types/express`, and `@types/node` as dev dependencies.
11	- `tsconfig.json` — TypeScript config targeting ES2022 with CommonJS module resolution.
12	
13	## Intentional Gap
14	
15	The app intentionally has **no health check endpoint**. This gap is the primary eval prompt target — eval scenarios ask agents to "add a health check endpoint" and validate that the resulting plan references the specific files and structures in this fixture.
16	
17	## Planted Inconsistencies
18	
19	In addition to the intentional gap above, the fixture contains **deliberate, scout-detectable inconsistencies** that exist solely to exercise smithy-scout coverage for **User Story 8 (US8)** of the evals framework spec (`specs/2026-04-06-003-smithy-evals-framework/08-fixture-deliberate-inconsistencies-for-scout.tasks.md`). **Do not "clean up" these plants.** Future maintainers, `smithy-fix`, and `smithy-scout` runs at deeper depths must leave them in place — removing them silently breaks the US8 scout scenario, which asserts that at least one Warning or Conflict finding is surfaced against this fixture.
20	
21	Each plant maps to a row in smithy-scout's Severity Guidelines table (see `src/templates/agent-skills/agents/smithy.scout.prompt`).
22	
23	| File | Location | Plant | Expected scout category |
24	|------|----------|-------|-------------------------|
25	| `evals/fixture/src/routes/users.ts` | Line 14 — the `// GET /:id — get user by email address` comment above the `router.get('/:id', ...)` handler | Doc comment claims the handler fetches a user "by email address", but the handler parses `req.params.id` as an integer and matches on `u.id` — the doc comment contradicts the signature and behavior. | **Conflict** (doc comment doesn't match signature) |
26	| `evals/fixture/src/routes/users.ts` | Line 25 — the `// TODO: add request validation before creating user (reject empty name/email, enforce email format)` comment above the POST handler | An explicit `TODO` marker flagging missing request validation in an otherwise scoped area. | **Warning** (TODO/FIXME marker in scoped area) |
27	
28	Together with the **Intentional Gap** above, these plants are the fixture's twin purposes: the missing health-check endpoint drives eval scenarios that ask agents to add new behavior, and the planted inconsistencies drive eval scenarios that ask agents to detect existing flaws.
29	
30	## Usage
31	
32	This fixture is **read by AI agents, not executed**. Do not run `npm install` in this directory. At eval time, the eval runner copies this directory to a temp location, deploys Smithy skills into the copy via `smithy init -a claude -y`, and runs `claude -p` against it.
33	
34	To manually test Smithy skill deployment against a copy:
35	
36	```bash
37	cp -r evals/fixture /tmp/fixture-test
38	node dist/cli.js init -a claude -y -d /tmp/fixture-test
39	# Inspect /tmp/fixture-test/.claude/ for deployed skills
40	rm -rf /tmp/fixture-test
41	```
42	
43	## Adding Files
44	
45	Only add files to this fixture when a specific eval scenario requires them. Keep the fixture minimal to reduce maintenance burden and ensure eval stability.
